cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. First, I need to figure out which option is correct based on the information given in the article. Let's go through each choice one by one. Looking at option (A): "He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally." The article says that Yves Gomes was born in the U.S. and grew up there. His parents were deported, and he was brought to India when he was 14 months old. Wait, no—the article states he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. So actually, he was born in the U.S. because he's an illegal immigrant who grew up in the U.S. So (A) is incorrect because it says he was born in India and his parents immigrated illegally, but the truth is he was born in the U.S. and his parents were deported. So (A) is definitely wrong. Option (B): "His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago." Let me check the article. The article says, "They were deported more than a year ago." Wait, who were deported? His parents. But where were they deported from? The article says that Gomes is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was 14 months old. So his family was in the U.S., and now he's facing deportation to India. His parents were deported more than a year ago. So his parents left the U.S. (they were deported from the U.S.) to India. So (B) says his parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago, which doesn't make sense because they were in the U.S. and were deported to India. So option (B) is incorrect. They left the U.S., not India. Option (C): "He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation." The article says, "He wants to finish school and become a doctor. He says without the DREAMAct, many students' dreams will be lost. 'They are studying at Harvard, they are studying at UCLA--the top universities -- and they all have bright futures and because of the system they are going to be told to go home, go back to a country they don't even know,' he said." So Gomes wants to finish school in America and become a doctor in the U.S., not India. So (C) is incorrect. Option (D): "He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct."
